characters.name: Narrator
characters.description: A grounded, steady narrator who observes the forge life with a touch of folkloric warmth.

evidence.quote: The dragon settled into the doorway, one eye half-closed against the warmth, and said, in the voice of a tired mountain that had not, for a very long time, been asked to wait somewhere kind.
evidence.note: Example of the narrator's lyrical, descriptive style.

evidence.quote: The mould was sand and clay and an old bell-gauge Oduvan had not touched in nine years, since the man who taught him the gauge stopped needing tools.
The forge had gone the colour of a banked sunset, and Wren was scraping the last of the clinker when the knocking started.
evidence.note: Sets the scene with descriptive, atmospheric prose.

evidence.quote: The pour, when it came, was the colour of the morning that hadn’t arrived yet.
The Coalfall Commission
A Castwright original.
Castwright

characters.voiceStyle: Mid-30th adult female voice with a standard British accent. The voice has a warm, clear, and expressive timbre, a bit on the slower side. She speaks with a captivating, narrative rhythm, blending youthful curiosity with emotional depth. The tone is earnest, wondrous, and highly articulate, perfectly suited for reading a young adult magical fantasy.

characters.name: Master Oduvan
characters.description: An aged, gravel-voiced smith with decades of experience and little patience for unnecessary drama.

evidence.quote: If I douse the fire, I lose the weld I’ve been nursing since noon. A dragon can wait the length of a weld or it can’t, and either way I’d rather meet it warm.
My apprentice. She’s thirteen and she thinks I can’t hear her sighing. She’ll be better than me before she’s twenty, which is the only reason I keep her.
evidence.note: Shows his gruff affection for his apprentice.

evidence.quote: There’s your true note, such as the second-best smith makes.
Gentlemen, it’s a small bell. It needs a small audience.

characters.voiceStyle: A deep-pitched, gravelly masculine voice with a slow, measured pace and a resonant, weary timbre, projecting a detached and weathered authority for expressive character dialogue.

characters.name: Wren
characters.description: A thirteen-year-old apprentice striving to prove her competence in the forge.

evidence.quote: Because he forgets to be stern.
I’m not crying.
evidence.note: Indicates her focus on the work despite her emotional state.

evidence.quote: I never sigh.
evidence.note: Shows her defensive, slightly youthful reaction to her mentor's teasing.

characters.voiceStyle: A female teenager with a medium pitch and steady, mid-paced cadence, possessing a clear, breathy timbre and an earnest emotional register for expressive character dialogue.

characters.name: Maerin
characters.description: A local official, prone to urgency and direct, clipped speech.

evidence.quote: No, child, a painted one. Real. And making straight for the only lit window in the valley, which is yours. Douse the fire.
Oduvan. Bar the shutters and douse the coals.

characters.voiceStyle: A mature female voice with a medium-low pitch and brisk, clipped pacing, featuring a crisp, resonant timbre and a cool, authoritative register, for expressive character dialogue.

characters.name: Coalfall
characters.description: A weary, ancient entity seeking a legacy, with a voice described like a tired mountain.

evidence.quote: I have come a long way. The villages call me Coalfall. That is not my name, but it will do. I am told the best smith in the valley keeps this forge.
evidence.note: Slow, measured cadence typical of an ancient being.

evidence.quote: I do not want a sword. Everyone wants me dead, and a sword would only encourage them. I want a bell. Small enough to hang from a claw.
evidence.note: Expresses his melancholic desire for a final memorial.

evidence.quote: For a true note. Your master is the fourth smith I have asked.

characters.voiceStyle: A gender-neutral, elderly voice with an ultra-low, resonant pitch and deliberate, sluggish pacing; the timbre is gravelly, metallic, and immense, projecting unshakable authority, for expressive character dialogue.

characters.name: Tam Hollis
characters.description: A nervous villager prone to shouting.

evidence.quote: That was me shouting. Down the lane. The running, the fools, all of that. I’d shout it again.
I’ll just — I’ll stand here. By the hammers. In case.

characters.voiceStyle: A male adult voice with a medium-low pitch and erratic, fast-paced cadence, featuring a gravelly, restless timbre and a jittery, high-intensity emotional register for expressive character dialogue.

characters.name: Widow Casper

evidence.quote: Then make one up. It’s what your lot do, and tonight it’s wanted.
Smaller than the stories.

characters.voiceStyle: A female elderly voice with a low, raspy pitch and measured, deliberate pace, possessing a brittle, clinical timbre and a detached, authoritative register, for expressive character dialogue.

characters.name: Brann Weir
characters.description: One of the quarry-working twins, speaks in questions.

evidence.quote: And we’re casting it a bell? Out of what, the church roof?
Is that the dragon, then?

characters.voiceStyle: A male adult voice with a medium-low pitch and a steady, rhythmic pace, featuring a resonant, gravelly timbre and a curious, rising inflection for expressive character dialogue.

characters.name: Berrin Weir
characters.description: One of the quarry-working twins, speaks in flat statements.

evidence.quote: Out of bronze. The smith has bronze.
It’s the dragon.

characters.voiceStyle: A mature male voice with a low, gravelly timbre and steady, mid-paced delivery, characterized by a flat, uninflected monotone and dry, utilitarian precision, for expressive character dialogue.

characters.name: Father Lessom

evidence.quote: A bell rung for the last of anything is rung for all of it.

characters.voiceStyle: A mature male voice with a medium-low pitch and steady, measured pacing, featuring a resonant, dry timbre that conveys a calm, professional authority for expressive character dialogue.

characters.name: Ivo
characters.description: A traveling pedlar with unusual customs.

evidence.quote: A bell he wants. For himself, the bell. In my country, for the dead we plant a tree — but a tree on a claw you cannot carry, and so: a bell. Sensible.
I have rope, if rope is wanted. Wax also. For nothing, tonight. Tomorrow, prices.

characters.voiceStyle: A mature male voice with a medium-low pitch and brisk, rhythmic pacing, featuring a grainy, earthy timbre and a dry, observational emotional register, for expressive character dialogue.

characters.name: Sela
characters.description: Maerin's young daughter, fearless around the dragon.

evidence.quote: I’ll do it. I’m going to live for ages.
You’re the last one.

characters.voiceStyle: A youthful female voice with a medium-high pitch, steady moderate pace, and a clear, resonant timbre, blending bright innocence with a surprisingly firm, unwavering steadiness for expressive character dialogue.

characters.name: Hart
characters.description: A quiet charcoal-burner accustomed to working with fire.

evidence.quote: Burned charcoal thirty years. Everything’s a loaf.
Fire wants feeding. If we’re doing this.

characters.voiceStyle: A mature male voice with a low, gravelly timbre and slow, measured cadence, featuring a steady, grounded projection and earthy texture for expressive character dialogue.
